description: Facing a single open from a non-blind seat: 3-bet (value+bluff) / call / fold, by hero position x opener position. Linear/value-heavy per low-stakes research.

actions.combos: TT, JJ, AQs, AJs, KQs, KJs, QJs

entries.rationale: UTG opens the strongest range at the table and UTG+1 sits directly behind it with the whole field still to act, so this is the tightest vs-RFI spot in the pack: 3-bet only genuine value (QQ+, AK) plus the A5s blocker-bluff, and flat a narrow high-equity band (TT-JJ, AQs-AJs, suited Broadways) — dominated offsuit hands and thin suited hands can't continue profitably against a range this strong, and cold-calling with 5+ players still to act invites a squeeze, so the flat stays disciplined.

actions.combos: 99, TT, JJ, AQs, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs

entries.rationale: UTG+2 sits one seat closer to the button than UTG+1, so its continuing range widens a touch: the pair floor drops to 99 and the call range picks up ATs/JTs, since one fewer player behind means less squeeze exposure — the 3-bet range stays the same genuine-value-plus-blocker shape because UTG's open is still the strongest range at the table.

actions.combos: 99, TT, JJ, AQs, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs

entries.rationale: UTG+1's open is only a hair wider than UTG's and still EP-strong, so UTG+2 re-values essentially as it does facing UTG: 3-bet QQ+/AK plus the A5s blocker, flat the same 99-JJ/suited-Broadway band — the one-notch-wider opener doesn't justify loosening further against a value-heavy pool that rarely opens EP light.

actions.combos: 77-JJ, AQs, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs

entries.rationale: UTG's range is the strongest at the table, so HJ 3-bets only genuine value (QQ+, AK) plus the A5s blocker bluff and calls a tight set of pairs and suited broadways — dominated offsuit hands and thin suited hands don't have enough equity to continue profitably against a range this strong.

actions.combos: 66-JJ, AQs, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs, T9s

entries.rationale: CO's extra seat of position over HJ lets the calling range widen down to 66 and add T9s: the players who could squeeze behind CO have already folded, so speculative suited hands can realize more of their equity in position against UTG's tight range.

actions.combos: 66-TT, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs, AQo

entries.rationale: HJ opens wider than UTG, so CO's continuing range loosens to match: the 3-bet threshold drops to JJ/AQs and the call range picks up AQo, since a looser opener makes offsuit broadway hands profitable to continue with in position.

actions.combos: TT+, AQs, AKs, AKo, A5s, A4s
22-99, AJs, ATs, A9s, KJs, KTs, QJs, QTs, JTs, T9s, 98s, AQo, AJo, KQo

entries.rationale: CO's open is wide enough that BTN can 3-bet down to TT and call with any pair down to 22 plus offsuit broadways like AJo/KQo — BTN's positional advantage lets these speculative and dominated-adjacent hands realize their equity postflop that they couldn't from an earlier seat.

actions.combos: 22-TT, AJs, ATs, KJs, QJs, JTs, T9s, AQo

entries.rationale: HJ's open is tighter than CO's, so BTN's continuing range shrinks accordingly: the 3-bet threshold rises to JJ+ and the weakest suited connectors drop out of the call range, since a stronger opening range makes those speculative calls less profitable even in position.

Facing the tightest possible open, BTN's continuing range is its narrowest vs-RFI spot: only QQ+/AK 3-bet for value and a handful of pairs plus AQs/AJs/KQs call, because UTG's range is strong enough that even BTN's positional edge doesn't rescue weaker holdings.
